replication_study.hypothesis: Within a U.S. state-year panel (2007-2016), the elasticity of carbon-dioxide emissions with respect to average weekly working hours is positive: a 1 % increase in working hours is associated with a > 0 % increase in CO₂ emissions, net of economic, demographic, and political covariates.

data_plan.source_type: Administrative / secondary data aggregated from U.S. EPA, BLS, BEA, Census, League of Conservation Voters
data_plan.sample_size: ≈ 500 state-year observations (50 states × 10 years, subject to missingness)
data_plan.access_details: Files located in /app/data/original/7/input/replication_data; unrestricted public sources, already compiled.

qualification.explanation: The data are constructed from the same public sources as the original study, but extend the time-series. This provides an independent time segment to test robustness of the focal claim.
qualification.similarity_to_original: Variables wrkhrs (average weekly working hours), epa (state CO₂ emissions), and all listed controls match those described in post_registration.json (original data section) and are loaded by Fitzgerald 2018 Script_clean v2.R, ensuring measurement equivalence.
qualification.deviation_from_original: The compiled panel includes years 2014-2016 (year codes 14-16) which were not in the original 2007-2013 sample (post_registration.json, data.wave_or_subset). Thus the replication adds three new years, increasing N by ~150 and providing a partially new sample.

data_plan.notes: The .dta files are read in R; they must reside inside /app/data when the script is executed. Year is coded as integers 7-16, so 2000+year gives the calendar year. All continuous variables are log-transformed in the script prior to modelling.

planned_method.steps:
1. Load compiled.dta, epa.dta, hhsize.dta.
2. Melt hhsize to long format and merge with main panel.
3. Merge EPA emissions.
4. Construct derived variables: employed-population %, manufacturing % of GDP, log-transform listed variables.
5. Estimate Prais-Winsten two-way fixed-effects panel model with panel-corrected standard errors and AR(1) correction using panelAR().
6. Repeat model on (a) full 2007-2016 data, (b) original-years subset (2007-2013), (c) new-years subset (2014-2016) for robustness.
7. Extract coefficient for wrkhrs and its standard error.
8. Compare magnitude, direction, and statistical significance to original estimate (0.668).
planned_method.models: Prais-Winsten two-way fixed-effects panel regression with AR(1) correction (panelAR, panelCorrMethod='pcse').
planned_method.outcome_variable: Log of state-level CO₂ emissions from fossil-fuel combustion (epa).
planned_method.independent_variables: Log of average weekly working hours per worker (wrkhrs).
planned_method.control_variables: Log labor productivity (laborprod), log population (pop), log energy production (energy), log avg household size (hhsize), log percent employed population (emppop_pct), log manufacturing share of GDP (manu_gdp), log working-age population (workpop), state fixed effects, year fixed effects.
planned_method.tools_software: R (>=4.1) with packages: readstata13, tidyverse, reshape2, prais, panelAR.

planned_estimation_and_test.estimation: Elasticity coefficient of wrkhrs on epa.
planned_estimation_and_test.test: t-test (reported by panelAR) on H0: β_wrkhrs = 0 (two-sided).

planned_method.missing_data_handling: panelAR complete.case=TRUE → listwise deletion within model.
planned_method.inference_criteria: Replicated if coefficient is positive and p < 0.05; directional consistency and magnitude within ±25 % of 0.668 regarded as strong replication.

files.Fitzgerald 2018 Script_clean v2.R: End-to-end replication script. Loads three .dta files, constructs variables, runs panelAR models (models 1-6), and prints summaries.

codebase.notes: Paths inside the script must remain relative; when executed inside the container, working directory should be set so that compiled.dta, epa.dta, hhsize.dta are found in ./data. No additional code modifications anticipated.

analysis.instructions: In the container, run: Rscript 'Fitzgerald 2018 Script_clean v2.R'. Ensure working directory contains replication_data and data are in /app/data. Results will print to console; capture into a log for record.
analysis.comparison_metrics:
1. Sign of β_wrkhrs.
2. p-value (<0.05 threshold).
3. Elasticity magnitude: |β_replication – 0.668| / 0.668.
4. 95 % confidence interval overlap with original point estimate.
